zh.lifeSummary: 當Aubrey II de Vere於大約1065年出生在英國英格蘭艾可卡素 丁加姆時，他的父親Aubrey de Vere I36歲，母親Beatrice de Ghent34歲。他於1108年之前與Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1141年5月15日在英國倫敦享年77歲，埋葬在英國艾可易阿爾斯 可爾內。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aubrey II de Vere于大约1065年出生在英国英格兰艾可卡素 丁加姆时，他的父亲Aubrey de Vere I36岁，母亲Beatrice de Ghent34岁。他于1108年之前与Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1141年5月15日在英国倫敦享年77岁，埋葬在英国艾可易阿爾斯 可爾內。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Aubrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

variants.name: Aubree
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Aubrey II de Vere was born about 1065, in Castle Hedingham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, Aubrey de Vere I, was 36 and his mother, Beatrice de Ghent, was 34. He married Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare before 1108, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 15 May 1141, in London, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Earls Colne, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aubrey II de Vere est né environ 1065, à Castle Hedingham, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Aubrey de Vere I, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Ghent, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare avant 1108, à Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 15 mai 1141, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Earls Colne, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aubrey II de Vere um 1065 in Castle Hedingham, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Aubrey de Vere I 36 und seine Mutter Beatrice de Ghent 34. Er hat vor 1108 in Suffolk, England Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 15. Mai 1141 im Alter von 77 Jahren in London, England und wurde in Earls Colne, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aubrey II de Vere è nato il circa 1065 nel Castle Hedingham, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Aubrey de Vere I, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice de Ghent, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare prima del 1108, nel Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 15 maggio 1141, nel London, England, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolto a Earls Colne, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Aubrey II de Vereが約1065年にイギリスイギリスエセックスカストル ヘディンガムで生まれた時，父Aubrey de Vere Iは36歳，母Beatrice de Ghentは34歳であった。1108年以前にイギリススフォルクでAdeliza FitzGilbert de Clareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1141年5月15日にイギリスロンドンで77歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスエアルルズ コルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aubrey II de Vere(이)가 대략 1065년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 카스틀 헤딩함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Aubrey de Vere I(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice de Ghent(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1108년 이전 영국 수폴크에서 Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1141년 5월 15일 영국 런던에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 에를스 코니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aubrey II de Vere nasceu aproximadamente 1065, em Castle Hedingham, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Aubrey de Vere I, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice de Ghent, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare antes 1108, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 15 de maio de 1141, em London, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aubrey II de Vere родился около 1065 г. в местности кастл хедингхам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Aubrey de Vere I было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Ghent, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare перед 1108 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 15 мая 1141 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности эарлс колн, эссекс, Англия.

Se casó con Adeliza FitzGilbert de Clare antes de 1108, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1141, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultado en Earls Colne, Essex, Inglaterra.
